title: Introduction to PartMI
text: Part Mutual Information (PMI) quantifies direct association two variables XX YY removing influence set conditioning variables ZZ: PMI(X;Y|Z)=∑x,y,zP(x,y,z)log⁡P(x,y|z)P(x|z)P(y|z) PMI(X; Y | Z) = \sum_{x,y,z} P(x,y,z) \log \frac{P(x,y|z)}{P(x|z)P(y|z)} mathematically equivalent Conditional Mutual Information (CMI), applied specifically problem direct association network inference.

title: Introduction to PartMI
text: Consider three genes , B, C regulates B B regulates C. Standard correlation mutual information show false positive association C (indirect edge). PMI conditioning B correctly identifies direct -C link.

title: Introduction to PartMI
Introduction to PartMI
code: library(PartMI) set.seed(42)  # Independent variables x <- rnorm(200) y <- rnorm(200) mi(x, y)  # near zero #> [1] 0.2549785  # Dependent variables y_dep <- x + rnorm(200, 0, 0.3) mi(x, y_dep)  # positive value #> [1] 1.113316

title: Introduction to PartMI
code: # Common cause scenario z <- rnorm(200) x <- 0.7 * z + rnorm(200, 0, 0.5) y <- 0.7 * z + rnorm(200, 0, 0.5)  # MI shows spurious association mi_xy <- mi(x, y) mi_xy #> [1] 0.3968508  # PMI removes the common cause pmi_xy <- pmi(x, y, z) pmi_xy #>         1  #> 0.1104176

title: Introduction to PartMI
code: # Simulate a small regulatory network n_obs <- 100 TF1  <- rnorm(n_obs) TF2  <- rnorm(n_obs) Gene1 <- 0.8 * TF1 + rnorm(n_obs, 0, 0.4) Gene2 <- 0.6 * TF2 + rnorm(n_obs, 0, 0.4) Gene3 <- 0.5 * Gene1 + 0.3 * TF2 + rnorm(n_obs, 0, 0.3) Noise <- rnorm(n_obs)  dat <- data.frame(TF1, TF2, Gene1, Gene2, Gene3, Noise)  net <- pmi_network(dat, n_permutations = 50, threshold = 0.05) #> Computing PMI for 6 variables... #>   Pair 1/15 (7%) #>   Pair 2/15 (13%) #>   Pair 3/15 (20%) #>   Pair 4/15 (27%) #>   Pair 5/15 (33%) #>   Pair 6/15 (40%) #>   Pair 7/15 (47%) #>   Pair 8/15 (53%) #>   Pair 9/15 (60%) #>   Pair 10/15 (67%) #>   Pair 11/15 (73%) #>   Pair 12/15 (80%) #>   Pair 13/15 (87%) #>   Pair 14/15 (93%) #>   Pair 15/15 (100%) #> Found 2 edges at threshold 0.050 (fdr corrected).  # View the adjacency matrix print(net$adjacency) #>       TF1 TF2 Gene1 Gene2 Gene3 Noise #> TF1     0   0     1     0     0     0 #> TF2     0   0     0     1     0     0 #> Gene1   1   0     0     0     0     0 #> Gene2   0   1     0     0     0     0 #> Gene3   0   0     0     0     0     0 #> Noise   0   0     0     0     0     0  # Summary print(net) #> PMI Network #>   Method:            binning  #>   Threshold:         0.05  #>   Adjusted p-value:  fdr  #>   Permutations:      50  #>   Nodes:             6  #>   Edges:             2  #>   Density:           0.1333

title: Introduction to PartMI
text: Fast histogram-based estimator. Good exploratory analysis. Control granularity bins parameter.

title: Introduction to PartMI
text: accurate, especially higher-dimensional Z. Based Frenzel-Pompe estimator. Control bias-variance tradeoff k.

title: Introduction to PartMI
Introduction to PartMI
text: Zhao et al. (2016) “Part mutual information quantifying direct associations networks.” PNAS 113(18): 5130-5135. Frenzel & Pompe (2007) “Partial Mutual Information Coupling Analysis Multivariate Time Series.” Physical Review Letters 99(20): 204101. Kraskov, Stögbauer & Grassberger (2004) “Estimating mutual information.” Physical Review E 69(6): 066138.

Non-linear: Unlike standard correlation, catches complex biological “curves.” “False Links”: specifically quantifies direct connection. Low Power Requirements: require supercomputer GPU. uses basic math (logarithms probability distributions) standard laptop CPU can calculate seconds. possible use publicly available data TCGA (Cancer Genome Atlas) GEO (Gene Expression Omnibus), using Step--Step Approach: Download Data: Grab simple CSV file gene expression levels specific disease (e.g., Lung Cancer). Define Variables: Let XX Gene 1, YY Gene 2, ZZ “background” genes. Calculate PMI: Instead standard Mutual Information formula: (X;Y)=∑∑P(x,y)log⁡P(x,y)P(x)P(y)(X; Y) = \sum \sum P(x, y) \log \frac{P(x, y)}{P(x)P(y)}  package already implements PMI formula, uses “partial” joint probability. allows see XX YY actually talking , just look like ZZ talking .

what: Why this hasn’t been widely implemented yet (I think!)
text: biologists rely “--shelf” software like WGCNA (Weighted Gene Co-expression Network Analysis). WGCNA old uses simple correlation. , creating user-friendly, lightweight software tool replaces WGCNA PMI major contribution field. solves “limitation indirect edges” frequently cited papers, uses open data, runs perfectly normal home/office PC.

what: Part Mutual Information for Direct Association Network Inference
title: Part Mutual Information for Direct Association Network Inference
text: {Part Mutual Information Direct Association Networks}

title: Part Mutual Information for Direct Association Network Inference
text: PartMI implements Part Mutual Information (PMI) inferring direct associations biological networks. Unlike standard correlation mutual information (MI), PMI conditions background variables remove indirect dependencies, substantially reducing false-positive edges.

title: Part Mutual Information for Direct Association Network Inference
Part Mutual Information for Direct Association Network Inference
code: library(PartMI)  # Simulate expression data set.seed(42) n <- 200 z <- rnorm(n)                    # hidden regulator x <- 0.7 * z + rnorm(n, 0, 0.5) # gene X y <- 0.7 * z + rnorm(n, 0, 0.5) # gene Y (indirect via Z)  # MI picks up the indirect association mi(x, y)  # > 0 (false positive)  # PMI removes the common cause Z pmi(x, y, z)  # ~ 0 (correct)

title: Part Mutual Information for Direct Association Network Inference

Frenzel & Pompe (2007) Partial Mutual Information Coupling Analysis Multivariate Time Series. Phys. Rev. Lett. 99:204101. Kraskov, Stögbauer & Grassberger (2004) Estimating mutual information. Phys. Rev. E 69:066138.

what: PartMI: Part Mutual Information for Direct Association Network Inference — PartMI-package
title: PartMI: Part Mutual Information for Direct Association Network Inference — PartMI-package
text: Implements Part Mutual Information (PMI) inferring direct associations biological networks. PMI extends standard Mutual Information (MI) conditioning background variables, removing indirect dependencies reducing false-positive edges network reconstruction.

title: PartMI: Part Mutual Information for Direct Association Network Inference — PartMI-package
text: mi - Compute mutual information two variables pmi - Compute part (conditional) mutual information pmi_network - Infer direct association network normalize_data - Normalize expression data discretize_data - Discretize continuous data

what: Estimate Mutual Information (MI) between two variables — mi
title: Estimate Mutual Information (MI) between two variables — mi
text: Computes mutual information (X; Y) using either binning-based histogram estimator k-nearest-neighbor (KSG) estimator.

title: Estimate Mutual Information (MI) between two variables — mi
code: mi(x, y, method = c("binning", "knn"), bins = 10, k = 3, ...)

text: x Numeric vector (first variable). y Numeric vector (second variable). method Estimation method: "binning" (default) "knn". bins Number bins per dimension binning method (default 10). k Number nearest neighbors kNN method (default 3). ... Additional arguments (currently unused).

Normalize expression data — normalize_data
text: Applies quantile normalization z-score normalization expression matrices. Designed gene expression data (rows = samples, columns = genes).

title: Normalize expression data — normalize_data
Normalize expression data — normalize_data
text: data Numeric matrix data.frame (samples x variables). method Normalization method: "zscore" (default): Center scale column mean 0, sd 1. "rank": Transform normal scores via rank-based inverse normal. "minmax": Scale column [0, 1]. "quantile": Quantile normalize across columns (samples must aligned; .e., rows matched observations). ... Additional arguments (currently unused).

title: Normalize expression data — normalize_data
text: Normalized matrix dimensions.

title: Normalize expression data — normalize_data
code: set.seed(42) dat <- matrix(rexp(200), nrow = 20, ncol = 10) colnames(dat) <- paste0("Gene", 1:10) norm_z <- normalize_data(dat, "zscore") norm_r <- normalize_data(dat, "rank") norm_q <- normalize_data(dat, "quantile")

what: Estimate Part Mutual Information (PMI) between X and Y given Z — pmi
title: Estimate Part Mutual Information (PMI) between X and Y given Z — pmi
text: Computes PMI(X; Y | Z), quantifying direct association X Y removing influence Z. Equivalent conditional mutual information (CMI) applied direct network inference.

title: Estimate Part Mutual Information (PMI) between X and Y given Z — pmi
code: pmi(x, y, z, method = c("binning", "knn"), bins = 5, k = 3, ...)

title: Estimate Part Mutual Information (PMI) between X and Y given Z — pmi
text: x Numeric vector (first variable). y Numeric vector (second variable). z Numeric vector, matrix, data.frame conditioning variable(s). column treated separate conditioning variable. method Estimation method: "binning" (default) "knn". bins Number bins per dimension binning (default 5). Decrease higher-dimensional Z avoid data sparsity. k Number nearest neighbors kNN method (default 3). ... Additional arguments (currently unused).

title: Estimate Part Mutual Information (PMI) between X and Y given Z — pmi
text: numeric PMI value nats.

title: Estimate Part Mutual Information (PMI) between X and Y given Z — pmi
text: PMI defined : $$PMI(X;Y|Z) = \sum_{x,y,z} P(x,y,z) \log \frac{P(x,y|z)}{P(x|z)P(y|z)}$$ Two estimators provided: "binning": histogram-based estimator (fast, good small Z) "knn": k-nearest-neighbor estimator (Frenzel-Pompe, robust higher-dimensional Z)

title: Estimate Part Mutual Information (PMI) between X and Y given Z — pmi
text: Zhao et al. (2016) PNAS 113(18):5130-5135. Frenzel & Pompe (2007) Phys. Rev. Lett. 99:204101.

title: Estimate Part Mutual Information (PMI) between X and Y given Z — pmi
code: set.seed(42) x <- rnorm(100) z <- rnorm(100) y <- x + z + rnorm(100, 0, 0.5) # PMI between x and y conditioning on z should be small pmi(x, y, z) #>         1  #> 0.6292994  # MI (without conditioning) will typically be larger mi(x, y) #> [1] 0.7356992

what: Infer a direct association network using Part Mutual Information — pmi_network
title: Infer a direct association network using Part Mutual Information — pmi_network
text: Constructs network edges represent direct associations variables. pair (, j), PMI computed conditioning variables (full-order conditioning) specified subset. Statistical significance assessed via permutation testing.

title: Infer a direct association network using Part Mutual Information — pmi_network
code: pmi_network(   data,   method = c("binning", "knn"),   bins = 5,   k = 3,   threshold = 0.05,   n_permutations = 100,   adjust = c("fdr", "bonferroni", "none"),   mc_cores = 1,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Infer a direct association network using Part Mutual Information — pmi_network
text: data Numeric matrix data.frame (rows = samples, columns = variables). Variable/column names used node labels. method PMI estimation method: "binning" "knn". bins Number bins binning estimator (default 5). k Number neighbors kNN estimator (default 3). threshold Significance threshold p-values (default 0.05). n_permutations Number permutations significance testing (default 100). Use least 1000 published results. adjust Method multiple testing correction: "none", "bonferroni", "fdr" (default "fdr"). mc_cores Number cores parallel computation (default 1). Windows users use 1. verbose Print progress messages (default TRUE). ... Additional arguments passed pmi.

title: Infer a direct association network using Part Mutual Information — pmi_network
text: list components: adjacency Adjacency matrix (binary, 0/1) significant edges. pmi_matrix Matrix PMI values pairs. pvalue_matrix Matrix permutation p-values. pvalue_adjusted Matrix adjusted p-values.

title: Infer a direct association network using Part Mutual Information — pmi_network
Infer a direct association network using Part Mutual Information — pmi_network
code: set.seed(42) n <- 50 g <- rnorm(n) x <- g + rnorm(n, 0, 0.5) y <- g + rnorm(n, 0, 0.5) z <- x + rnorm(n, 0, 0.3) w <- rnorm(n) dat <- data.frame(X = x, Y = y, Z = z, W = w) net <- pmi_network(dat, n_permutations = 20, threshold = 0.1) #> Computing PMI for 4 variables... #>   Pair 1/6 (17%) #>   Pair 2/6 (33%) #>   Pair 3/6 (50%) #>   Pair 4/6 (67%) #>   Pair 5/6 (83%) #>   Pair 6/6 (100%) #> Found 1 edges at threshold 0.100 (fdr corrected). print(net$adjacency) #>   X Y Z W #> X 0 0 1 0 #> Y 0 0 0 0 #> Z 1 0 0 0 #> W 0 0 0 0
